title: galphimia brasiliensis var. platyphylla
intro: Galphimia brasiliensis var. platyphylla, also known as Mexican gentian, is a plant species that belongs to the family Malpighiaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Galphimia brasiliensis var. platyphylla include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, where it is valued for its expectorant and tonic properties.
